data.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide is expected to ride AI initiatives to improved productivity
data.description:
C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) motored higher on Monday after Wells Fargo upgraded the stock to an Overweight rating after having it slotted at Equal-weight.

Analyst Chris Wetherbee and his team see a unique opportunity for earnings to compound through 2027, driven by improved execution through tech initiatives. At the company's upcoming investor day, CHRW management is expected to outline that productivity can improve through the implementation of new technology and AI.

"Automation should drive more productivity gains and support a return to ~40% NAST operating margins through the cycle. We expect this to be a key pillar of guidance, and it's a driver of our ~$7.50 in '27 EPS bull case scenario."

Over the long term, the opportunity is seen being even bigger, as AI potentially allows for step function gains in productivity of 30% to 50%. Notably, Wells Fargo's bull case price target on CHRW of ~$7.50 is almost 30% higher than the Street.

Shares of C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) were up 2.28% at 1:33 p.m. to trade within 5% of the 52-week share price high. Wall Street and Seeking Alpha analysts have a consensus Hold rating on CHRW.

data.title: T-Mobile CEO warns investors of risks in second half of Q4
data.description:
Shares of T-Mobile (NASDAQ:TMUS) turned defensive Monday after the company’s CEO warned of risks to the business in the second half of the current quarter.

“It's been a great quarter so far. The only thing I would caution, and I can just hear myself saying this in prior years as well, is that this quarter's back end loaded. So, while I'm here to tell you it's going great, we're confident in our guidance, investors should be cautious because there's a lot of risk in the back half,” said CEO Michael Sievert at a UBS conference, adding that device upgrade rate has been “pretty low.”

Late October, the company said it expected postpaid net customer additions to be between 5.6M and 5.8M, revised from the prior guidance of 5.4M to 5.7M and adjusted EBITDA between $31.6B and $31.8B versus a consensus estimate of $31.6B.

T-Mobile (TMUS) shares are down more than 5%, challenging support at the 50-day moving average.

data.title: AMD says report of AWS 'not yet' seeing heavy demand is 'not accurate'
data.description:
AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) said last week's report that contained comments made by an Amazon Web Services (NASDAQ:AMZN) executives that it has "not yet" seen heavy demand for the former's artificial intelligence accelerators is "not accurate."

"We have a great relationship with AWS and the report was not accurate - we are actively engaged with AWS and end customers on AI opportunities," an AMD spokesperson told Seeking Alpha via email on Monday.

Shares of the Dr. Lisa Su-led AMD fell 5% in midday trading on Monday, as investors expressed concern over comments made by Gadi Hutt, who works at Amazon's semiconductor unit Annapurna Labs.

"If customers have strong indications that those are needed, then there's no reason not to deploy," Hutt told Business Insider last week. Hutt, who is Annapurna's Director of Product and Customer engineering, added that Amazon Web Services has "not yet" seen high demand for AMD's chips.

Separately on Monday, Bank of America downgraded AMD shares due in part to the story, as well as concerns about the company's standing in the PC space.

AMD showed off its first AI accelerator last December, the MI300x. The newest version, the MI325X, is on track to be sent to customers in the current quarter.

The company upped its 2024 guidance in October and said it would generate $5B in revenue from its AI accelerators, up from a previous view of $4.5B.

data.title: Juniper Networks gains after report about DOJ review of HPE deal
data.description:
Juniper Networks (NYSE:JNPR) rose 1.7% on Monday after a report about the Department of Justice's review of its planned $14 billion sale to Hewlett Packard Enterprises.

Recent questions about the combination appear to be routine and didn't seem to be a concern for deal approval according to traders, who cited a MLex report from Friday after the close. The companies have agreed not to close the deal while the DOJ continues its review through Donald Trump's inauguration. A final decision from the antitrust regulator isn't expected before Trump takes office on Jan. 20.

HPE said on Thursday that both companies are working with the DOG as the agency continues its review of the deal, which is expected to close in the early part of 2025.

The latest update comes after a report last week that the Juniper (JNPR) sale will likely be pushed into next year. Investors are likely hoping that a DOJ decision may be pushed to the Trump administration, which some feel may be more amenable to deal approvals.

data.title: Twilio snaps six straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Twilio (NYSE:TWLO) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock was down 2.9% at $110.08 on Monday.

The information technology stock advanced 8.5% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has surged 49.5% so far this year, above the 27.7% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

TWLO is up 19% over the past one month.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, TWLO has a Strong Buy rating with a score of 4.95 out of 5. The company received B in the prospect of profitability, A in momentum, while it got A- in growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 15 analysts gave TWLO a Buy and above. 12 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while three recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also positive and see the stock as a Buy.

Earlier in November, Wells Fargo upgraded Twilio on its new build cycle. The analysts noted that they believe Twilio can serve as a pick-and-shovel play for the next wave of AI-native front office and communications-powered generative AI applications.

“The company's growth revitalization plan, driven by AI-enhanced product features and cost-cutting measures, is proving effective,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: VEON approves launch of initial $30M phase of share buyback program
data.description:
• VEON (NASDAQ:VEON) on Monday announced the commencement of the first phase of its share buyback program with respect to the company’s American Depositary Shares.
  • This first phase of the buyback will be in the amount of up to $30 million.
  • This $30 million first phase is part of VEON’s larger plan to execute a share buyback program of up to $100 million.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fangdd Network prices $7M stock offering
data.description:
• Fangdd Network Group (NASDAQ:DUO) has priced an offering of US$7M of Class A ordinary shares, or pre-funded warrants in lieu thereof, at a price of US$0.7 a share.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about Dec. 10, 2024, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • DUO shares were up 58% as of 1PM ET.

data.title: BQE Water to renew normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• BQE Water (TSXV:BQE:CA) said the TSX Venture Exchange has approved the firm's notice to to renew the normal course issuer bid.
  • Under the bid, the firm intends to buyback up to 64.1K shares.

data.title: CN reaches new tentative agreement with Unifor
data.description:
• CN said on Monday that it has reached a new tentative four-year collective agreement with Unifor. The union represents approximately 3,300 employees at CN in Canada, working in different mechanical, clerical, and intermodal functions.
  • No details of the tentative agreement will be released publicly until the agreement is ratified. The current collective agreement with Unifor expires on December 31, 2024.

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq decline from record highs, with Nvidia losing ground; Dow slips
data.description:
Major U.S. stock averages fell Monday, with market behemoth Nvidia (NVDA) emerging as a source of downward pressure after large-cap stocks marked record highs and gains for the previous week.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.4%, the Dow (DJI) -0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.4%. The benchmark (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) logged record-closing peaks on Friday, aided by accelerating expectations for another rate cut by the Federal Reserve. The Dow last week marked its first-ever close above 45,000.

Investors came into Monday's session seeing a fresh Street-high 2025 target for the S&P 500 (SP500), with Oppenheimer initiating its view of 7,100. But on its way to locking in double-digit gains for 2024, nine of the S&P 500's (SP500) 11 sectors were in the red on Monday. Communication Services paced decliners by losing -1.1%, and Consumer Discretionary, last week's top-performing sector, slipped. Energy and Materials were the two advancers.

AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA), part of all three key indexes, was a notable decliner Monday, down -2.7%, but off session lows. China's antitrust agency reportedly opened an investigation into the second most-valuable publicly traded company on concerns it may have breached monopoly laws. The news was first reported domestically by Bloomberg.

Nvidia (NVDA) said in a statement that it "wins on merit," and that it was "happy to answer any questions regulators may have" about its business.

Meanwhile, separate deal-related developments for Interpublic Group of Companies (IPG) and Hershey (HSY) propelled shares in each to the top of the S&P 500 (SP500).

Hershey (HSY) +15% after Bloomberg reported that Mondelez (MDLZ) is evaluating a potential takeover of the chocolate maker. Mondelez (MDLZ) fell -2.2%. Interpublic (IPG) +10% after the company agreed to be purchased by rival advertising agency Omnicom (NYSE:OMC). Omnicom tumbled -8.3%.

Investors are keeping in sight consumer and wholesale inflation reports due later this week. The November consumer price index will land on Wednesday, while the producer price index will be released on the day after.

"A further surprise for 2025 would be a hike from the Federal Reserve and events like Wednesday’s U.S. CPI will be a key determinant even if the near-term implication will be whether the Fed cut next week or not," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. The Fed will meet on December 17-18.

In the fixed-income market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.19%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.13%.

In global news, the rule of the Assad family in Syria ended after 50 years as rebels took control of Damascus on Sunday.
